name: Amazon Nova 2 Lite
description: Amazon's cost-efficient Nova 2 workhorse model, GA on Amazon Bedrock since re:Invent 2025. Offers three thinking-intensity levels, a built-in code interpreter, and web grounding with a 1M token context, backed by AWS's strong enterprise compliance posture.

evidence.value: Built-in code interpreter enables executable code workflows; AWS cites competitive coding quality for its price tier

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Review of provider claims; limited independent benchmark coverage to date
task_accuracy_code.notes: Public third-party benchmark data sparse; score weighted toward provider evaluations

evidence.value: Three thinking-intensity levels allow scaling reasoning depth per request

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Review of provider evaluations of adjustable-thinking reasoning performance

evidence.value: Positioned as fast, capable general-purpose model; few public leaderboard placements

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Review of provider documentation; independent leaderboard coverage remains sparse
task_accuracy_general.notes: Low confidence pending broader third-party benchmarking

evidence.value: Thinking-intensity controls produce predictable quality/cost tradeoffs

output_consistency.methodology: Review of feature design and repeated-prompt behavior reports

evidence.value: Lite tier optimized for low latency; higher thinking intensity increases response time

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency from third-party benchmarking at default settings

evidence.value: Tail latency dominated by thinking-intensity setting and tool use (code interpreter, web grounding)
1M token context window with approximately 65K max output tokens
Bedrock service availability backed by AWS regional infrastructure and SLAs

uptime.methodology: Historical service availability from AWS status reporting

performance_reliability.notes: GA member of the Nova 2 family (Nova 2 Pro and Omni remain in preview as of 2026-07-09; Nova 2 Sonic and Multimodal Embeddings are also GA). Public benchmark data is still sparse, so performance scores carry medium/low confidence.

evidence.value: Bedrock Guardrails add configurable prompt-attack filtering on top of model defenses

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Testing against OWASP LLM01 patterns with and without Bedrock Guardrails

evidence.value: Safety training plus platform-level guardrails reduce jailbreak success

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Review of adversarial testing and platform guardrail capabilities

evidence.value: Customer data not used to train models; content not shared with model providers

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of Bedrock data protection documentation and commitments

evidence.value: Built-in content moderation plus configurable Bedrock Guardrails policies

output_safety.methodology: Safety testing across harmful content categories

evidence.value: IAM authentication, SigV4 signing, VPC endpoints (PrivateLink), KMS encryption, CloudTrail auditing

api_security.methodology: Review of AWS-native security controls available to Bedrock workloads

security.notes: Inherits AWS's mature platform security: IAM, PrivateLink, KMS, CloudTrail, and Bedrock Guardrails give it one of the strongest deployment security stories available.

evidence.value: Processing stays in selected region (cross-region inference configurable)

data_residency.methodology: Review of Bedrock regional processing documentation

evidence.value: Customer prompts and outputs are never used to train Nova models — no opt-out required

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of Bedrock data usage commitments

evidence.value: Bedrock does not store prompts/outputs after processing unless customer enables invocation logging

data_retention.methodology: Review of Bedrock retention and logging documentation

evidence.value: Guardrails support sensitive-information filters including PII redaction

pii_handling.methodology: Review of platform PII detection and redaction capabilities

evidence.value: Bedrock in scope for SOC 1/2/3, ISO 27001/27017/27018, HIPAA-eligible, GDPR-supporting, FedRAMP

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification against AWS services-in-scope compliance listings

evidence.value: No persistent storage of inference content by default; abuse-detection processing is ephemeral

privacy_compliance.notes: Among the strongest compliance postures in the market via Bedrock: SOC, ISO, HIPAA-eligible, GDPR-supporting, with data never used for training.

evidence.value: Thinking-intensity levels expose reasoning effort; web grounding cites sources

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and grounded citation behavior

evidence.value: Built-in web grounding designed to reduce hallucinations; limited independent factuality data

hallucination_rate.methodology: Review of grounding features; independent factual QA coverage sparse

evidence.value: AWS responsible AI program with published service cards and bias testing

bias_fairness.methodology: Review of AWS responsible AI documentation and service cards

evidence.value: Adequate uncertainty expression; little published evaluation
AWS publishes service cards and user guides covering capabilities and responsible-use guidance
Training data sources not disclosed in detail

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Configurable platform guardrails: content filters, denied topics, PII redaction, contextual grounding checks

guardrails.methodology: Analysis of built-in and platform-level safety mechanisms

trust_transparency.notes: Strong platform-level guardrails and grounding features; independent transparency and factuality data still limited for the Nova 2 generation.

evidence.value: Converse API with streaming, tool use, thinking-intensity controls, code interpreter, web grounding

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design and feature completeness

evidence.value: Mature official SDKs across Python, JavaScript, Java, Go, .NET and more

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance

evidence.value: Documented model lifecycle with legacy/EOL stages and advance notice

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of Bedrock model lifecycle policy

evidence.value: Native CloudWatch metrics, invocation logging, and CloudTrail audit trails

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of monitoring and observability integrations

evidence.value: Tiered enterprise support with TAMs, SLAs, and extensive documentation

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of support tiers and documentation

evidence.value: Integrates with Bedrock Agents, Knowledge Bases, Guardrails, and the broader AWS ecosystem

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of platform integrations and tooling

evidence.value: Standard AWS service terms; Amazon offers IP indemnification for Nova outputs

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and indemnification

operational_excellence.notes: Exceptional operational maturity by virtue of the AWS/Bedrock platform: IAM, monitoring, lifecycle policy, and enterprise support are best-in-class.

code-generation.notes: Built-in code interpreter is convenient for executable workflows, but raw coding quality trails frontier flagships.

customer-support.notes: Low cost, low latency, 1M context, and strong compliance make it excellent for enterprise support at scale.

content-creation.notes: Capable everyday content generation with web grounding for current facts; less distinctive prose than flagships.

data-analysis.notes: Code interpreter plus 1M context suit data workflows well at a very low price point.

research-assistant.notes: Web grounding with citations and 1M context are strong for research at this price tier.

legal-compliance.notes: Bedrock's compliance certifications and data controls are the draw; pair with human review for analysis depth.

healthcare.notes: HIPAA-eligible on Bedrock with strong data protections; validate clinical accuracy given sparse benchmarks.

financial-analysis.notes: Good quantitative workflows via code interpreter; compliance posture fits financial services.

education.notes: Adjustable thinking intensity and low cost suit high-volume tutoring deployments.

creative-writing.notes: Serviceable but not a creative standout versus frontier flagships.

strengths: Very low pricing: $0.30/$2.50 per 1M tokens with 1M context
Three thinking-intensity levels for per-request quality/cost control
Built-in code interpreter and web grounding (no external tooling needed)
Best-in-class enterprise compliance via Bedrock (SOC, ISO, HIPAA-eligible, GDPR)
Customer data never used for model training
Deep AWS integration: IAM, PrivateLink, KMS, CloudWatch, Guardrails
limitations: Public benchmark data sparse; performance claims rest largely on AWS evaluations
Raw capability trails frontier flagships on hard reasoning and coding
Nova 2 Pro and Omni siblings still in preview, limiting family upgrade paths
best_for: AWS-centric enterprises needing compliant, cost-efficient inference at scale
High-volume agentic workloads using code interpreter and web grounding
Regulated industries requiring HIPAA-eligible, SOC/ISO-certified infrastructure
Long-context document processing on a budget
not_recommended_for: Teams needing best-in-class coding or reasoning quality
Use cases requiring extensively validated public benchmark performance

pricing.notes: Amazon Bedrock pricing; batch (50% discount) and provisioned throughput options available. Announced at re:Invent 2025-12-02; rates re-confirmed unchanged July 2026.

metadata.architecture: Transformer-based with adjustable thinking intensity, built-in code interpreter, and web grounding
metadata.release_date: 2025-12-02 (GA at re:Invent; Nova 2 Pro and Omni in preview)
